name: Ingest webhook payloads with PostgreSQL on autopilot

parameters.query: SELECT * FROM payloads WHERE updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'source' on incoming payload.

parameters.text: =✅ Ingest webhook payloads with PostgreSQL on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09341-payload`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL)

### Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL)

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Required Fields Present? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.source }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Work in Batches · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Work in Batches

Loop controller — releases 5 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Aggregate Batch Results · ②→ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/payloads — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Respect Rate Limits · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

### Mark Completed in Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Sort Payloads Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Sort Payloads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Ingest webhook payloads with PostgreSQL on autopilot

**Pattern:** Webhook Pipeline · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Ingest webhook payloads with PostgreSQL on autopilot. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual backups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Pending Queue (PostgreSQL) → Required Fields Present? → Work in Batches → Aggregate Batch Results → Mark Completed in Google Sheets → Sort Payloads Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to webhook pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating backups in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your backups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
